name: ITIL 4
description: IT service management best practice framework with 34 management practices organized into general, service, and technical categories.
license_note: Metadata only. No AXELOS copyrighted text reproduced.

items.title: Architecture management
items.description: Provides an understanding of the different elements that make up an organization and how those elements relate to one another. Enables the organization to plan change effectively by mapping technology, information, and processes in a structured way. Supports agility by maintaining alignment between business strategy and IT infrastructure.
items.purpose: Provide an understanding of all the different elements that make up an organization and how those elements interrelate, enabling the organization to effectively achieve its current and future objectives.
kpis: Percentage of services aligned to enterprise architecture
Time to approve architecture change requests

items.title: Continual improvement
items.description: Aligns the organization's practices and services with changing business needs through ongoing identification and improvement of services, practices, and every element involved in effective management. Uses a structured approach including the continual improvement model to drive incremental and transformational gains. Embeds improvement thinking into the culture of the organization.
items.purpose: Align the organization's practices and services with changing business needs through the ongoing identification and improvement of services, service components, practices, or any element involved in the efficient and effective management of products and services.
kpis: Percentage of improvements completed on schedule
Staff participation rate in improvement activities

items.title: Information security management
items.description: Protects the information needed by the organization to conduct its business, covering confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data. Manages security risks through policies, processes, and controls. Balances prevention, detection, and correction to ensure an appropriate security posture aligned to business needs and regulatory requirements.
items.purpose: Protect the information needed by the organization to conduct its business, including understanding and managing risks to the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information and related assets.
kpis: Number of security incidents per period
Percentage of staff completing security awareness training
Time to detect and respond to security events

items.title: Knowledge management
items.description: Maintains and improves the effective, efficient, and convenient use of information and knowledge across the organization. Ensures that stakeholders have the right information in the right format at the right time. Reduces duplication of effort and enables faster decision-making through shared knowledge repositories and structured knowledge transfer processes.
items.purpose: Maintain and improve the effective, efficient, and convenient use of information and knowledge across the organization.
kpis: First-contact resolution rate enabled by knowledge

items.title: Measurement and reporting
items.description: Supports good decision-making and continual improvement by providing relevant data in a format that is meaningful to stakeholders. Defines which metrics and key performance indicators are relevant for different audiences and ensures timely, accurate reporting. Reduces noise in data and focuses attention on what matters most for service performance and business outcomes.
items.purpose: Support good decision-making and continual improvement by decreasing the levels of uncertainty through the collection of relevant data on various managed objects and valid assessment of this data in an appropriate context.
kpis: Percentage of KPIs with automated data collection

items.title: Organizational change management
items.description: Ensures that changes in an organization are implemented smoothly and that lasting benefits are achieved by managing the people side of change. Addresses resistance, builds stakeholder engagement, and supports adoption of new ways of working. Distinct from IT change enablement, which focuses on technical changes to services and infrastructure.
items.purpose: Ensure that changes in an organization are smoothly and successfully implemented, and that lasting benefits are achieved by managing the human aspects of the changes.
kpis: Training completion rate for organizational changes

items.title: Portfolio management
items.description: Ensures that the organization has the right mix of programmes, projects, products, and services to execute its strategy within funding and resource constraints. Prioritizes investments, balances risk and value, and provides visibility into the total portfolio of work. Enables leaders to make informed decisions about where to allocate resources for maximum business impact.
items.purpose: Ensure that the organization has the right mix of programmes, projects, products, and services to execute the organization's strategy within its funding and resource constraints.
kpis: Number of in-flight projects vs. capacity

items.title: Project management
items.description: Ensures that all projects in the organization are delivered successfully by applying appropriate governance, planning, and control methods. Covers initiation, planning, execution, monitoring, and closure of projects. May follow waterfall, agile, or hybrid approaches depending on organizational needs and the nature of the initiative.
items.purpose: Ensure that all projects in the organization are successfully delivered, meeting stakeholder expectations for time, cost, quality, and scope.

items.title: Relationship management
items.description: Establishes and nurtures the links between the organization and its stakeholders at strategic and tactical levels. Identifies, analyzes, monitors, and continually improves relationships with stakeholders. Ensures that the service provider understands stakeholder needs and that feedback channels exist to maintain trust and mutual value.
items.purpose: Establish and nurture the links between the organization and its stakeholders at strategic and tactical levels.
kpis: Number of escalations from key stakeholders

items.title: Risk management
items.description: Ensures that the organization understands and effectively handles risks to maintain the viability of the organization and create value. Covers risk identification, assessment, treatment, and monitoring. Integrates with enterprise risk management approaches and ensures that risk appetite is understood and applied to IT decision-making.
items.purpose: Ensure that the organization understands and effectively handles risks, protecting the organization from loss while enabling value creation.
kpis: Number of risks identified and assessed

items.title: Service financial management
items.description: Supports the organization's strategies and plans for service management by ensuring that financial resources and investments are being used effectively. Covers budgeting, accounting, and charging for IT services. Provides transparency on cost of services and enables informed financial decision-making for IT investments and operations.
items.purpose: Support the organization's strategies and plans for service management by ensuring that the organization's financial resources and investments are being used effectively.
kpis: IT spending as percentage of revenue

items.title: Strategy management
items.description: Formulates the goals of the organization and adopts the courses of action and allocation of resources necessary for achieving those goals. Defines the organization's direction, positioning, and approach to achieving its vision. Ensures that IT strategy is aligned with and supports the overall business strategy.
items.purpose: Formulate the goals of the organization and adopt the courses of action and allocation of resources necessary for achieving those goals.

items.title: Supplier management
items.description: Ensures that the organization's suppliers and their performance are managed appropriately to support the seamless provision of quality products and services. Covers supplier evaluation, selection, contract management, and performance monitoring. Manages risks in the supply chain and ensures value realization from third-party relationships.
items.purpose: Ensure that the organization's suppliers and their performance are managed appropriately to support the seamless provision of quality products and services.

items.title: Workforce and talent management
items.description: Ensures that the organization has the right people with the appropriate skills and knowledge in the correct roles to support its business objectives. Covers workforce planning, recruitment, competency development, performance management, and succession planning. Builds the human capabilities required to deliver and support IT services effectively.
items.purpose: Ensure that the organization has the right people with the appropriate skills and knowledge and in the correct roles to support its business objectives.

items.title: Availability management
items.description: Ensures that services deliver agreed levels of availability to meet the needs of customers and users. Defines, analyzes, plans, measures, and improves all aspects of the availability of IT services. Covers proactive design for availability, reactive monitoring, and reporting against availability targets.
items.purpose: Ensure that services deliver agreed levels of availability to meet the needs of customers and users.

items.title: Business analysis
items.description: Analyzes a business or some element of a business, defines its associated needs, and recommends solutions to address those needs or solve business problems. Creates value by bridging the gap between business stakeholders and IT. Covers requirements gathering, process analysis, and solution evaluation to ensure IT investments deliver intended business outcomes.
items.purpose: Analyze a business or some element of a business, define its associated needs, and recommend solutions to address those needs and/or solve a business problem, thereby facilitating value creation.

items.title: Capacity and performance management
items.description: Ensures that services achieve agreed and expected performance levels, satisfying current and future demand in a cost-effective way. Covers performance monitoring, capacity planning, demand management, and workload modeling. Proactively identifies capacity constraints before they impact service quality.
items.purpose: Ensure that services achieve agreed and expected performance, satisfying current and future demand in a cost-effective way.

items.title: Change enablement
items.description: Maximizes the number of successful service and product changes by ensuring that risks are properly assessed, authorizing changes to proceed, and managing the change schedule. Balances the need for rapid change with the need to manage risk. Classifies changes as standard, normal, or emergency to apply appropriate levels of control and governance.
items.purpose: Maximize the number of successful service and product changes by ensuring that risks have been properly assessed, authorizing changes to proceed, and managing the change schedule.

items.title: Incident management
items.description: Minimizes the negative impact of incidents by restoring normal service operation as quickly as possible. Covers incident detection, logging, categorization, prioritization, diagnosis, and resolution. Differentiates between major incidents requiring dedicated response and standard incidents handled through normal workflows.
items.purpose: Minimize the negative impact of incidents by restoring normal service operation as quickly as possible.
inputs: Incident reports from users or monitoring

items.title: IT asset management
items.description: Plans and manages the full lifecycle of all IT assets to help the organization maximize value, control costs, manage risks, support decision-making about purchase and reuse, and meet regulatory and contractual requirements. Covers hardware, software, cloud assets, and licensing. Ensures accurate asset inventory for operational and financial purposes.
items.purpose: Plan and manage the full lifecycle of all IT assets, to help the organization maximize value, control costs, manage risks, support decision-making, and meet regulatory and contractual requirements.

items.title: Monitoring and event management
items.description: Systematically observes services and service components, and records and reports selected changes of state identified as events. Categorizes events as informational, warning, or exception and determines appropriate responses. Provides the basis for automated operations and proactive service management through real-time visibility into infrastructure and application health.
items.purpose: Systematically observe services and service components, and record and report selected changes of state identified as events.

items.title: Problem management
items.description: Reduces the likelihood and impact of incidents by identifying actual and potential causes of incidents, and managing workarounds and known errors. Operates reactively by investigating recurring incidents and proactively by analyzing trends and identifying potential weaknesses. Maintains a known error database to enable faster incident resolution.
items.purpose: Reduce the likelihood and impact of incidents by identifying actual and potential causes of incidents, and managing workarounds and known errors.
outputs: Requests for change to resolve root causes

items.title: Release management
items.description: Makes new and changed services and features available for use in live environments. Plans, schedules, and controls the movement of releases through testing environments to the live environment. Ensures that the integrity of the live environment is protected and that the correct components are released together.
items.purpose: Make new and changed services and features available for use.

items.title: Service catalogue management
items.description: Provides a single source of consistent information on all services and service offerings, and ensures that it is available to the relevant audience. Maintains an accurate and up-to-date service catalogue that describes available services, their dependencies, and how to request them. Enables self-service and reduces unnecessary contacts to the service desk.
items.purpose: Provide a single source of consistent information on all services and service offerings, ensuring it is available to the relevant audience.

items.title: Service configuration management
items.description: Ensures that accurate and reliable information about the configuration of services and the configuration items that support them is available when and where it is needed. Maintains a configuration management database (CMDB) recording relationships between components. Supports change management, incident diagnosis, and impact assessment.
items.purpose: Ensure that accurate and reliable information about the configuration of services, and the CIs that support them, is available when and where it is needed.

items.title: Service continuity management
items.description: Ensures that the availability and performance of a service is maintained at sufficient levels in the event of a disaster. Plans for and manages disaster recovery capabilities including backup, restore, and failover mechanisms. Conducts business impact analysis to determine continuity requirements and tests recovery plans regularly.
items.purpose: Ensure that the availability and performance of a service is maintained at a sufficient level in the event of a disaster.
kpis: DR test frequency and success rate

items.title: Service design
items.description: Designs products and services that are fit for purpose, fit for use, and that can be delivered by the organization and its ecosystem. Considers all aspects of service design including architecture, processes, policies, documentation, and measurement. Produces service design packages that ensure new or changed services meet stakeholder requirements.
items.purpose: Design products and services that are fit for purpose, fit for use, and that can be delivered by the organization and its ecosystem.

items.title: Service desk
items.description: Captures demand for incident resolution and service requests, serving as the entry point and single point of contact for the service provider with all of its users. Provides a clear path for users to report issues, ask questions, and make requests. Can be organized as local, centralized, virtual, or follow-the-sun models.
items.purpose: Capture demand for incident resolution and service requests, being the entry point and single point of contact for the service provider with all of its users.

items.title: Service level management
items.description: Sets clear business-based targets for service levels, and ensures that delivery of services is properly assessed, monitored, and managed against these targets. Defines service level agreements (SLAs) that translate customer needs into measurable commitments. Drives improvements when service levels are not being met and validates that SLAs remain relevant.
items.purpose: Set clear business-based targets for service levels, and ensure that delivery of services is properly assessed, monitored, and managed against these targets.

items.title: Service request management
items.description: Supports the agreed quality of a service by handling all pre-defined, user-initiated service requests in an effective and user-friendly manner. Covers fulfillment of standard requests such as password resets, access provisioning, and information queries. Uses automation and self-service to improve speed and consistency of delivery.
items.purpose: Support the agreed quality of a service by handling all pre-defined, user-initiated service requests in an effective and user-friendly manner.

items.title: Service validation and testing
items.description: Ensures that new or changed products and services meet defined requirements before they are deployed to the live environment. Covers functional testing, performance testing, user acceptance testing, and regression testing. Validates that service levels and quality criteria will be met when the service goes live.
items.purpose: Ensure that new or changed products and services meet defined requirements.

items.title: Deployment management
items.description: Moves new or changed hardware, software, documentation, processes, or any other component to live environments. May also be involved in deploying components to other environments for testing or staging. Uses deployment approaches such as phased, big bang, continuous delivery, or blue-green deployments to manage risk and minimize disruption.
items.purpose: Move new or changed hardware, software, documentation, processes, or any other component to live environments.

items.title: Infrastructure and platform management
items.description: Oversees the technology infrastructure and platforms used by an organization, including management of physical and virtual resources such as servers, networks, storage, and cloud platforms. Ensures that infrastructure components are available, reliable, and optimized. Covers both on-premises and cloud environments, applying infrastructure-as-code and automation principles where appropriate.
items.purpose: Oversee the infrastructure and platforms used by an organization, enabling the monitoring of technology solutions available, including solutions from third parties.

items.title: Software development and management
items.description: Ensures that applications meet internal and external stakeholder needs, in terms of functionality, reliability, maintainability, compliance, and auditability. Covers the full software development lifecycle from requirements to retirement. Supports both custom development and configuration of commercial-off-the-shelf solutions, using agile, DevOps, or traditional development methods.
items.purpose: Ensure that applications meet stakeholder needs in terms of functionality, reliability, maintainability, compliance, and auditability.
